summary: The Varnado Group is an employee-benefits consulting and brokerage firm in Baton Rouge, Louisiana that helps small businesses (roughly 1-50 employees) build affordable, competitive benefits packages — group health, dental, vision, disability, cancer, and hospital indemnity coverage. The model is commission-based (carriers pay the broker, so consulting is cost-neutral to the employer), and the engagement is a relationship sale led personally by John Varnado, anchored on the annual open-enrollment and renewal cycle.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Bridge a newly signed client from 'agreement signed' to 'enrollment complete' — set expectations, reduce drop-off, and reinforce the decision so benefits actually go live.
campaigns.trigger: New client agreement signed / first contact-form inquiry that converts to an engagement.
campaigns.segment: Newly onboarded small-business clients; branch by whether they're a first-time-benefits employer or layering voluntary lines onto an existing plan.
campaigns.rationale: Varnado has no onboarding sequence today, so the high-stakes gap between signing and the first completed enrollment runs on ad-hoc outreach — exactly where a relationship-driven brokerage loses momentum and trust.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome the moment the agreement is signed: confirm the decision, lay out the enrollment roadmap, and give one clear first action so the process starts moving immediately.
emails.subject: Welcome aboard — here's exactly what happens next
emails.preview: Your enrollment roadmap (and the one thing we need from you first).
keyPoints: Reaffirm the choice: you're now set up to offer benefits that compete with the big employers — leveling the playing field
Lay out the simple step-by-step path from today to live coverage so nothing feels overwhelming
Single first action: return the employee census/roster so plan design and quoting can begin
Personal, human close from John — reply directly with any question, a real person reads every one

campaigns.name: Consultation-to-Client Conversion
campaigns.objective: Turn an inbound contact-form inquiry into a booked needs-assessment consultation and then a signed client, without letting leads go cold.
campaigns.trigger: Contact-form submission / phone inquiry expressing interest.
campaigns.segment: Active prospects who inquired but haven't yet booked or signed; branch by no-benefits vs. has-basic-benefits.
campaigns.rationale: With no lead magnet feeding the funnel, the inbound inquiry IS the top of funnel — and inquiries currently go cold without follow-up. This is the core revenue play and the most direct lever on Varnado's stated goal of more conversions.

emails.purpose: Respond instantly to an inquiry, dismantle the #1 'we're too small / can't afford it' objection, and drive a booked free needs-assessment call.
emails.subject: You're not too small for real employee benefits
emails.preview: A 20-minute call shows you what's actually within reach — at no cost to you.
keyPoints: Lead by naming the objection head-on: most owners assume good benefits are only for big companies — they're not
Explain the cost-neutral model: our consulting is paid by the carriers, not out of your budget
Lower the friction: the first step is a free 20-minute needs assessment, not a commitment
Local trust signal: a Baton Rouge advisor who guides the whole process personally

campaigns.name: Renewal, Open Enrollment & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Protect recurring revenue by proactively owning the annual open-enrollment window and grow accounts by adding voluntary benefit lines.
campaigns.trigger: Renewal date / plan-year end minus 90 days (open enrollment typically Oct-Jan for calendar-year plans).
campaigns.segment: Active clients approaching renewal; branch by account health (healthy = expansion offer, at-risk/low-engagement = save-and-re-onboard).
campaigns.rationale: The annual enrollment cycle is the single highest-lifetime-value lever and is currently completely unaddressed — clients switch brokers at renewal when they feel underserved, so proactive outreach here both defends and expands the book.

emails.purpose: Open the renewal conversation 90 days out with a value recap of the past plan year and a prompt to review options before the enrollment window closes.
emails.subject: Your benefits renewal is coming — let's get ahead of it
emails.preview: A quick recap of this year's coverage, plus what's worth revisiting for next year.
keyPoints: Recap the year: what coverage your team has, enrollment participation, and the value delivered
Frame why early matters: starting now means better options and no scramble before open enrollment
Plant the expansion seed: lines worth adding (dental, vision, disability, cancer) as the business grows
Reassure continuity: same personal advisor, proactively in your corner at renewal

campaigns.name: Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended consultations and enrollment meetings, and recover the ones that slip.
campaigns.trigger: Consultation or enrollment meeting booked; upcoming appointment; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Any prospect or client with a scheduled needs assessment, proposal review, or enrollment meeting.
campaigns.rationale: The entire Varnado sales motion runs on personally led calls (discovery, proposal, enrollment), so every missed meeting directly stalls revenue — reminders cut no-shows ~35% and protect the calls Campaign 2 works to book.

emails.purpose: Instantly confirm a booked consultation, set the calendar hold, and tell the prospect what to bring so the call is productive.
emails.subject: You're booked with John — here's what to have ready
emails.preview: Add it to your calendar and bring two quick things to make the call count.
keyPoints: Confirm date, time, and how the call happens (phone/video), with a one-click add-to-calendar
Set the prep: rough employee headcount and any current coverage details to bring
Set expectations: what we'll cover in the needs assessment and that it's no-obligation
Easy reschedule link so a conflict becomes a new time, not a no-show

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind and build authority across the long relationship cycle without discounting — keeping cold inquiries warm and existing clients loyal between renewals.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts — prospects and clients; suppress those in active onboarding and route the dormant to win-back.
campaigns.rationale: In a trust-driven, months-long benefits sale with a once-a-year decision point, an always-on educational newsletter is the engine that keeps Varnado present in owners' minds between the rare moments they're ready to act.

emails.purpose: Establish the recurring value habit with a practical, jargon-free piece that positions Varnado as the small-business owner's benefits advisor.
emails.subject: The benefit small employers overlook (and your competitors offer)
emails.preview: One low-cost coverage that punches above its weight for hiring and retention.
keyPoints: One useful idea per send: a plain-English explainer on a high-impact voluntary benefit (e.g., disability or hospital indemnity)
Tie it to the owner's real stakes — attracting and keeping talent against bigger competitors
80% value, 20% soft offer: a single low-friction CTA, no hard sell
Establish a recurring 'small-business benefits tip' section that builds the open habit
